title: Cephaelis brachypus
intro: Cephaelis brachypus, also known as Ixora brachypus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cephaelis brachypus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cephaelis brachypus is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make teas and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health problems.
taxonomy: Cephaelis brachypus is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is part of the genus Cephaelis, which is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as coffee of the forest. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes over 600 genera and more than 13,000 species. The classification of Cephaelis brachypus reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the Rubiaceae family.
